purpose: Ground truth labels for F2 summarisation evaluation. Each entry defines what a correct summary must contain for a real document in the database. Used by the RAGAS eval harness in Week 3.

labels.headline: CFPB issues final rule amending Regulation B (Equal Credit Opportunity Act) small business lending data collection requirements under Dodd-Frank Section 1071
labels.what_changed: CFPB revised Regulation B subpart B to amend: coverage of certain credit transactions and financial institutions; the small business definition; inclusion of certain data points; how data points are collected; and the compliance date.
labels.why_it_matters: Financial institutions that make small business loans must update their data collection systems and processes to comply with Section 1071 requirements. Non-compliance with ECOA data reporting exposes institutions to regulatory action.
affected_institution_types: any lender making small business loans
labels.notes: Full text is 400K+ chars (Pages 21620-21670 of Federal Register Vol 91 No 77). Compliance deadline is embedded in the rule text and may vary by institution size — summariser should flag this uncertainty.

entries.title: Small Business Lending Under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B)

labels.headline: CFPB proposes additional revisions to Regulation B small business lending rules to streamline compliance and improve data quality
labels.what_changed: Proposed amendments to Regulation B subpart B covering: credit transaction and institution coverage; small business definition; data point inclusion and collection methods; compliance date.
labels.why_it_matters: Community banks and credit unions making small business loans should review proposed changes and consider submitting comments. The proposal may further modify compliance timelines and data requirements already established in the April 2026 final rule.
labels.notes: This is a proposed rule — compliance_deadline is not applicable. Comment period deadline should be extracted if mentioned in full text.

entries.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of Atlantic Union Bank and former employee of Frost Bank

labels.headline: Federal Reserve issues consent prohibition orders against two former bank employees for CARES Act fraud and embezzlement
labels.what_changed: Fed issued consent prohibition orders barring Crystal Moore (former Atlantic Union Bank employee, CARES Act loan fraud) and Jesse Romo (former Frost Bank employee, embezzlement) from participating in banking.
labels.why_it_matters: Enforcement actions against employees signal Fed scrutiny of CARES Act loan programs and internal controls. Banks should review employee conduct monitoring and BSA/AML programs.
labels.notes: Individual enforcement actions against employees do not require institutional compliance action, but signal areas of regulatory focus. High confidence expected — facts are concrete and specific.

entries.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of Commerce Bank

labels.headline: Federal Reserve issues consent prohibition order against former Commerce Bank employee for fraudulent customer transactions
labels.what_changed: Fed issued consent prohibition order against Nakia R. Logan, former Commerce Bank (Kansas City, Missouri) employee, for fraudulent customer transactions.
labels.why_it_matters: Continued Fed enforcement actions for employee fraud signal ongoing attention to internal controls, customer account monitoring, and employee conduct programs.
labels.notes: Short document — high confidence appropriate. Fraudulent customer transactions is the stated reason.

entries.title: Agencies publish resolution plan feedback letters for certain domestic and foreign banking organizations

labels.headline: FDIC and Fed publish resolution plan feedback letters for large banks' July 2025 living will submissions
labels.what_changed: FDIC and Federal Reserve published feedback letters reviewing resolution plans (living wills) submitted in July 2025 by certain domestic and foreign banking organizations.
labels.why_it_matters: Institutions that submitted resolution plans should review feedback letters for required remediation actions. Living will deficiencies can trigger enforcement consequences for systemically important banks.
affected_institution_types: foreign banking organizations with US operations
labels.notes: Resolution plans apply to large institutions — community banks are generally exempt unless they grow above thresholds. Confidence may be lower because feedback letter content requires reading the attachments.

entries.title: Federal Reserve Board requests public comment on a proposal to establish a 'payment account'

labels.headline: Federal Reserve proposes new 'payment account' framework for eligible financial institutions to access Fed payment infrastructure
labels.what_changed: Fed proposed establishing a new 'payment account' category that legally eligible financial institutions could use specifically for clearing and settling payments, expanding access to Fed payment infrastructure.
labels.why_it_matters: Community banks and credit unions should evaluate whether the proposed payment account structure would benefit their payment operations and consider submitting public comments during the comment period.
labels.notes: Comment period deadline should be extracted from full text. This is a proposed rule — no compliance deadline yet.

entries.title: Privacy Act of 1974; System of Records

labels.headline: CFPB updates Privacy Act System of Records Notice
labels.what_changed: CFPB published an updated System of Records Notice (SORN) under the Privacy Act of 1974, describing categories of records maintained by the Bureau.
labels.why_it_matters: Minimal direct compliance impact for most financial institutions. Relevant for institutions that share consumer data with CFPB or are subject to CFPB examination — they should understand what records the CFPB maintains.
labels.notes: Low compliance impact document — expected confidence score may be lower because the document is administrative, not substantive rulemaking. A good summariser should flag 'low impact' accurately.

labels.headline: Federal Reserve holds federal funds rate steady at April 2026 FOMC meeting
labels.what_changed: FOMC decided to maintain the target range for the federal funds rate unchanged. Statement discusses economic conditions, inflation outlook, and balance sheet policy.
labels.why_it_matters: Stable rates affect funding costs, deposit pricing, and net interest margin for community banks. Institutions should review asset-liability management strategies in the context of the current rate environment.
labels.notes: FOMC statements are well-structured and factual — high confidence expected. Note: doc_id is null because this doc may not have been enriched yet — use URL match.

entries.title: Agencies issue host state loan-to-deposit ratios

labels.headline: Federal banking agencies publish annual host state loan-to-deposit ratios used to evaluate interstate branching compliance
labels.what_changed: Fed, OCC, and FDIC published updated host state loan-to-deposit ratios for use in evaluating whether interstate branches are meeting community credit needs under 12 USC 1831u(d).
labels.why_it_matters: Banks operating interstate branches must compare their own loan-to-deposit ratios to the published host state ratios. Ratios below the threshold trigger additional regulatory scrutiny of community reinvestment performance.
labels.notes: Technical regulatory notice — specific numbers (the ratios) should appear in summary.

entries.title: Federal Reserve Board issues Economic Well-Being of U.S. Households report

labels.headline: Federal Reserve publishes annual SHED report on economic well-being of US households
labels.what_changed: Fed released the annual Survey of Household Economics and Decisionmaking (SHED) report, covering financial resilience, banking access, housing, education debt, and retirement savings of US households.
labels.why_it_matters: SHED findings inform CFPB and Fed supervisory priorities. Community banks can use data to understand customer financial health, identify product opportunities, and support CRA performance.
labels.notes: Research report — lower compliance urgency. Summariser should correctly classify as 'informational' rather than action-required. Confidence floor is lower because extracting key findings from a long report is harder.

eval_instructions.faithfulness: Every claim in the AI summary must be supported by the source document text. Check: are there invented dates, thresholds, or institution names not in the original?
eval_instructions.relevance: Does the summary answer the compliance officer's question: 'What does this mean for my institution and what do I need to do?'
eval_instructions.doc_type_accuracy: The AI-classified doc_type must match the label. Misclassifying a proposed rule as a final rule is a high-severity error.
eval_instructions.date_extraction: effective_date and compliance_deadline must be exactly correct or null. An invented date is worse than null.
eval_instructions.passing_threshold: RAGAS faithfulness >= 0.85 AND answer_relevance >= 0.80 across all 10 entries.
